name: Holy Roman Empire (Imperial Banner, c.1400–1806)
description: A gold (yellow) field bearing a black double-headed eagle facing forward with wings displayed, the heraldic emblem of the Holy Roman Emperor.
history: The double-headed eagle on gold (Reichsadler) was used as the Imperial Banner of the Holy Roman Emperor from approximately 1400 (after Sigismund) until the empire's dissolution in 1806. The single-headed black eagle on gold predated this and remained associated with the German Kingship.
